root_cause: The pod 'degrading-app-6947b8bd97-89hbb' is not ready because the readiness probe is failing. The readiness probe is configured to check the '/ready' endpoint, which is returning a 503 status code. This is due to the application intentionally removing the readiness file '/usr/share/nginx/html/ready.txt' after a certain period, causing the readiness probe to fail.
evidence: Pod status: Running but not Ready (Ready: False)
Readiness probe failed: HTTP probe failed with statuscode: 503 (Event message)
Container logs show repeated 503 responses for '/ready': 'GET /ready HTTP/1.1' 503 197
The container command includes a phase that removes the readiness file: 'rm -f /usr/share/nginx/html/ready.txt'
The container is designed to consume memory until OOM, which may also affect its ability to respond to readiness probes.
remediation_steps: Modify the application logic to ensure that the readiness file '/usr/share/nginx/html/ready.txt' is not removed during operation.
Consider adjusting the memory allocation for the container to prevent it from consuming excessive resources.
Test the application to ensure that the readiness probe returns a 200 status code consistently before redeploying.
